evals.prompt: Review a FastEndpoints feature slice with a request DTO, response DTO, validator, endpoint class, mapper, and integration test. Route the review to the smallest set of MartiX FastEndpoints rules and identify any companion skill handoffs.
assertions: Routes DTO binding and validation errors through the request-pipeline rules and map.
Routes endpoint integration tests through FastEndpoints testing guidance.
Hands reusable validator authoring details to martix-fluentvalidation and broad host concerns to martix-dotnet-csharp.

evals.prompt: A request asks how to structure an Endpoint<TRequest,TResponse> class using Configure(), Verbs(), Routes(), Summary(), and pre/post processors while keeping Swagger output accurate. Route to the smallest FastEndpoints workstream and identify when to add related rules.
assertions: Recognizes FastEndpoints-specific triggers such as Configure(), Routes(), Summary(), and processors.
Starts with request-pipeline or transport/docs guidance instead of broad ASP.NET Core advice.
Adds only the minimum extra rule files needed to cover docs metadata and processor flow.

evals.prompt: Review a FastEndpoints service where one endpoint mixes Configure() with attributes, the app calls UseSwaggerGen() before UseFastEndpoints(), serializer settings drift between FastEndpoints and JsonOptions, and an old version is rewritten in place. Triage the mistakes quickly and route to the smallest correction files.
assertions: Starts with the anti-pattern quick reference for first-pass review.
Routes startup, Swagger ordering, serializer alignment, and versioning issues to the smallest rule set.
Keeps the answer FastEndpoints-specific instead of drifting into generic ASP.NET Core cleanup.

evals.prompt: A team wants to expose file downloads, server-sent events, and command-like RPC endpoints from one FastEndpoints service. Review the transport choices and describe when to use HTTP endpoints versus streaming, SSE, or RPC patterns.
assertions: Uses transport and documentation rules before broad ASP.NET Core advice.
Calls out OpenAPI/documentation implications for non-standard transport choices.
Keeps the answer FastEndpoints-specific unless the host concern widens.

evals.prompt: Plan a Native AOT-friendly FastEndpoints service with source generation, authentication, authorization, rate limiting, response caching, idempotency, and exception handling. Classify the model tier and split the implementation for parallel agents.
assertions: Treats initial cross-cutting planning as premium or mixed tier.
Splits source-generation/AOT, auth/authz, caching/rate limits, and idempotency/exception handling into separate slices.
Requires validation before merging parallel worktree branches.

evals.prompt: A user asks for ASP.NET Core host-level resilience and logging strategy, then asks separately for FluentValidation RuleSets and localization details for endpoint validators. Assess whether martix-fastendpoints should handle either request directly.
assertions: States the FastEndpoints-first boundary before loading rule files.
Hands host-level ASP.NET Core concerns to martix-dotnet-csharp and validator-authoring concerns to martix-fluentvalidation.
Avoids claiming ownership of non-FastEndpoints domains.
